title: False positive property assertion
body:
Basically I wrote a simple TB with a golden sample and a DUT. To compare the results of those two modules, I added the following assertion:
```
    assert property (@(posedge clk) divs_out1 == divs_out2);
```
which fails during simulation. However, when I take a look at the simulation trace, I see no mismatch in these two values.
Also, when I replace the assertion with the following code:
```
    always_ff @(posedge clk) begin
        if (divs_out1 != divs_out2) begin
            $display("DIVS failure, different outputs: out1=0x%0h, out2=0x%0h", divs_out1, divs_out2);
            $fatal();
        end
    end
```
The simulation completes successfully without a `$fatal()` being triggered.
So I might hit some bug here?


> Can you attach an example that shows the issue?

I have attached an example project to reproduce the issue.
Simple run `make` followed by `./sim_build/bitext_test/Vsim_bitext_test -t my_trace.fst`.
[example.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16784503/example.zip)

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

> What 'verilator --version' are you using?

```
Verilator 5.028 2024-08-21 rev v5.028
```
> Did you try it with the git master version?

I haven't tried the latest master commit yet.
